headline: Pendatang Narrative Persists Despite 6 Generations of Citizenship
context: A 2025 UKM study found 42% of Chinese and Indian Malaysians report being called 'pendatang' (immigrant) in the past year, despite families holding citizenship for up to 6 generations.

cards.big: 42% of Chinese and Indian Malaysians were called 'pendatang' in the past year.
cards.sub: Some families have held citizenship since 1957 — 69 years and 6 generations.

cards.big: Article 8(2) prohibits discrimination on grounds of race.
cards.sub: The 'pendatang' narrative contradicts constitutional equality but is not actionable under any specific law.

cards.big: 23 politicians used 'pendatang' rhetoric in public speeches between 2020 and 2025.
cards.sub: None faced party disciplinary action. 4 were subsequently promoted.

cards.big: 68% of Malay respondents in the same study said all citizens are equal regardless of ancestry.
cards.sub: The narrative is politically amplified beyond community sentiment.

cards.big: When 68% reject the narrative but 23 politicians promote it, the problem is not the public. It is the political incentive.
Citizenship is not provisional. A citizen of 6 generations is not a guest. Political language that suggests otherwise undermines the social contract it claims to defend.
